title: 이 꽃의 꽃말은?

goal.ko: 형용사 관형(예쁜 꽃)과 동사 현재 관형(좋아하는 꽃)으로 명사를 꾸며 말할 수 있어요!
goal.en: I can describe a noun using adjective modifiers (a pretty flower) and verb present modifiers (the flower I like)!

key_points: 형용사 어간 + -(으)ㄴ + 명사 — 예쁜 꽃, 멋진 꽃다발
동사 어간 + -는 + 명사 — 좋아하는 꽃, 먹는 음식

형용사 Descriptive Words.en: to be brave, sturdy (of a plant/person)

sections.title: -(으)ㄴ + 명사
sections.subtitle: 형용사 관형 — Describing a noun with an adjective
sections.description: To put a describing word right in front of a noun — like 'pretty flower' or 'cool bouquet' — attach -(으)ㄴ to the adjective stem. Remember 신 과일, 덜 익은 과일 from Episode 5? Same pattern — now it's official core grammar! 🎉

rule_boxes.label: 💡 Episode 5 콜백!
examples: 신 과일, 덜 익은 과일 — 그때 미리 봤던 문법이 이제 진짜 core로 돌아왔어요!

examples.english: A pretty lisianthus is in full bloom in the yard.
Shall we make a nice bouquet to give Mom?
Then what about this sturdy-looking flower?

sections.title: -는 + 명사
sections.subtitle: 동사 현재 관형 — Describing a noun with an action happening now
sections.description: To describe a noun using a verb — like 'the flower (Mom) likes' or 'the food (I) eat' — attach -는 to the verb stem. Unlike 형용사, 동사 always uses -는 no matter what — no 받침 rule to worry about!

rule_boxes.label: 🔵 받침 상관없이 항상 -는
examples: 가다 → 가는 학교 (the school [someone] goes to)

rule_boxes.label: ✅ 형용사 -(으)ㄴ vs 동사 -는
examples: 예쁜 꽃 (형용사 — what the flower is LIKE)
좋아하는 꽃 (동사 — what someone DOES to/with the flower)

examples.english: Let's make it with the flowers Mom likes.

lines.speaker: 리아
lines.en: Sis, a pretty lisianthus is in full bloom in the yard.

lines.speaker: 미래
lines.en: Shall we make a nice bouquet to give Mom?

lines.speaker: 리아
lines.en: Sounds good! Let's make it with the flowers Mom likes.

lines.speaker: 리아
lines.text: 언니, 이 꽃의 꽃말이 뭔지 알아?
lines.en: Sis, do you know what this flower's meaning is?

lines.speaker: 리아
lines.en: They say it's eternal love. Isn't that so romantic?

lines.speaker: 미래
lines.en: Then what about this sturdy-looking flower?

fill_items.en: A pretty lisianthus is in full bloom in the yard.
Shall we make a nice bouquet to give Mom?
Let's make it with the flowers Mom likes.
Then what about this sturdy-looking flower?

choices.en: Pretty flowers bloomed in the yard
The flowers all wilted in the yard
There are no flowers in the yard
They're making a bouquet with flowers Mom likes

step1_chunks.english: A pretty lisianthus is in full bloom in the yard.
Let's make it with the flowers Mom likes.
Do you know what this flower's meaning is?
step1_chunks.hint: 꽃말 = the meaning of a flower

chunks.en: do you know what it is?

step2_words.english: Pretty flowers are in full bloom in the yard.
Let's make it with the flowers Mom likes.
This flower's meaning is said to be eternal love.

write_items.question: 🌸 이 꽃, 색깔도 곱고 모양도 좋아요. 어떤 꽃이에요?
write_items.question_en: This flower has nice colors and a nice shape. What kind of flower is it?

write_items.question: 💐 엄마가 이 꽃을 제일 좋아해요. 어떤 꽃으로 만들까요?
write_items.question_en: Mom likes this flower the most. What flowers should we use?
write_items.answer_en: Let's make it with the flowers Mom likes.

lines.kr: 저 파란 옷이 제일 예쁜 것 같아. 내가 좋아하는 색이야!
lines.en: I think that blue one is the prettiest. It's the color I like!

lines.kr: 내가 좋아하는 음식은 떡볶이야! 맛있는 음식이지.
lines.en: The food I like is tteokbokki! It's a tasty food.

self_check.ko: 형용사 어간 + -(으)ㄴ으로 명사를 꾸밀 수 있어요. (예쁜 꽃)
self_check.en: I can use adjective + -(으)ㄴ to describe a noun (a pretty flower).

self_check.ko: 동사 어간 + -는으로 명사를 꾸밀 수 있어요. (좋아하는 꽃)
self_check.en: I can use verb + -는 to describe a noun (the flower I like).

self_check.ko: 형용사 관형과 동사 현재 관형의 차이를 알아요.
self_check.en: I know the difference between adjective modifiers and verb present modifiers.
I know 꽃, 꽃다발, 꽃말, and 만발하다.
